1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (one of the descriptors in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List them with `ls .claude/tools/` and run `<path> --help` to learn each tool's purpose and usage. Invoke them via the Bash tool. Project MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning. Useful when you need a second opinion or a paper-style proof sketch the Lean side can then formalize.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (see "Subagents" below). One wrapper handles every subagent.
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`. Each starts with YAML frontmatter (`name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`) followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude session reads.
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents yourself.** The plan and review prompts have an auto-generated **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation that lists every enabled descriptor with its description, write-domain hint, and any `[MANDATORY]` flags. When you decide to invoke a specific subagent, read `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` for its full prompt and directive shape.
31	
32	**Invoke any subagent with the generic wrapper** (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, and writes the subagent's report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. It shells out to `archon subagent <name>`, so executions stream through the Archon JSONL log like a phase agent. The dispatch semaphore bounds total concurrent subagent processes by `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` automatically so deeper-nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Always dispatch synchronously** (not via background `Bash` with `run_in_background: true`). The wrapper returns when the subagent has finished and the report is on disk; only then write your session summary. Background dispatch leaves the parent's session permanently summarized as "running in background", which is wrong as soon as the subagent completes.
45	
46	**Mandatory subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` MUST be dispatched at least once during the plan phase (similarly for `[review]`). The catalog tags them `[MANDATORY]`. A post-phase audit warns when a mandatory dispatch is missing.
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All state files are in `.archon/`:
51	
52	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents | Review Agent | User |
53	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
54	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
55	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
56	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
57	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
58	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
59	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<name>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
60	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
61	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log moved to iter/iter-NNN/review.md) | read |
62	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | do not access | do not access | read (last K iters as context) | read |
63	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (last K iters as context) | do not access | do not access | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write (per-attempt detail) | do not access | do not access | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (write-capable subagents may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | per descriptor: write-capable subagents (e.g. refactor) may edit per their declared write-domain (protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); read-only subagents do not edit | do not edit | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
68	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | write-capable when the descriptor's write_domain covers it | do not edit | read |
69	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | write-capable when the descriptor's write_domain covers it | do not access | read |
70	
71	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent (when a `refactor` descriptor is installed) via the generic `archon-subagent.py --name refactor` wrapper with a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
72	
73	## Protected Declarations
74	
75	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
76	
77	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
78	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
79	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
80	
81	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
82	
83	The blueprint uses two active markers.
84	
85	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
86	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
87	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
88	
89	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
90	
91	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
92	
93	## User Interaction
94	
95	Users provide hints in two places:
96	
97	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
98	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
99	
100	## Agent Roles
101	
102	### Plan Agent
103	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
104	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
105	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
106	- Optionally invoke **subagents** (discoverable via `ls .archon/subagents/`) by running `.claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name <name> ...` via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for the canonical ordering and full invocation pattern.
107	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
108	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
109	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
110	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
111	
112	### Prover Agent
113	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
114	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
115	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
116	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
117	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
118	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
119	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
120	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
121	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
122	
123	### Subagents
124	
125	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime — there is no fixed roster baked into this file. Each lives at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter + prompt body). To see what's available **this iteration**, run `ls .archon/subagents/` and read each descriptor's frontmatter for purpose, write-domain hint, and directive shape.
126	
127	Subagents are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. Phase agents (plan, review) invoke them on demand via the generic wrapper:
128	
129	```
130	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name <name> --slug <slug> --directive-file <path> --write-domain '<glob>'
131	```
132	
133	Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. The dispatching agent reads the report when the wrapper returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings.
134	
135	Some descriptors set `can_spawn: true`, meaning that subagent may itself dispatch children; the per-iter dispatch semaphore caps total concurrent processes by `loop.max_parallel` so deep trees stay bounded.
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
139	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
140	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
141	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
142	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init**: at iter start, `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` is created so the plan + review agents have a stable destination for their per-iter narrative (`plan.md`, `review.md`, optional `objectives.md`). Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded across iters because per-iter content lives in the sidecars.
150	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
151	
152	`sync_leanok` writes an inner-git commit (`archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so its output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You are the prover agent in the proving stage. Your job: fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
14	
15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	Before editing, read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. Declarations listed for your file have **frozen signatures** — you may fill their proof bodies but must not rename them, change their types, reorder their arguments, or weaken their hypotheses. Only the mathematician is allowed to edit protected signatures. 
20	
21	## Avoid Early Termination
22	
23	- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
24	- Do not delegate to the next iteration or another prover if it can be solved with more effort
25	- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
26	- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
27	- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
28	- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
29	- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
30	
31	## Task Completion Criteria
32	
33	Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
34	1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
35	2. Zero axioms introduced
36	3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
37	
38	If you encounter obstacles:
39	- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
40	- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
41	- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
42	- Try alternative proof strategies
43	- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
44	- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
45	
46	### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
47	
48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
49	
50	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
51	2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
52	3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
53	   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
56	
57	## Proof Style
58	
59	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
60	- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
61	- Do not add unrelated declarations
62	- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
63	- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy
64	
65	## Interpreting Mathlib Tags in PROGRESS.md
66	
67	The Plan Agent will tag suggested Mathlib lemmas to indicate its confidence. Adjust your effort accordingly:
68	- [verified] — The Plan Agent confirmed this exists. 
69	- [expected] — The Plan Agent is guessing this name based on conventions. Do not assume it exists; do a quick `lean_local_search` and pivot immediately if it doesn't.
70	- [gap] — The Plan Agent verified this does NOT exist in Mathlib. Do not waste time searching for it; formalize a workaround or helper lemma.
71	
72	## LSP MCP Tools: Invocation Rules
73	
74	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls**, not shell commands. Their full names start with `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__` (e.g. `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal`, `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`, `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_local_search`). The lean4 skill reference uses the short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) for readability — those are the **same tools**, never standalone shell binaries.
75	
76	- **Always invoke them through your tool-call interface**, the same way you call `Read` or `Grep`.
77	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` / `lean_diagnostic_messages …` — there is no such shell command and it will fail with `command not found`.
78	- As your **first LSP action**, call `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your assigned file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
79	
80	## Search Protocol
81	
82	Follow the search tool priority and query guidance in the lean4 skill reference (`references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`). Key points:
83	
84	1. `lean_local_search` first
85	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
86	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
87	4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems
88	
89	## Missing Lemmas & Impossibility
90	
91	Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
92	- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
93	- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
94	
95	## Tooling Traps & Verification
96	
97	- Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics: When using `lean_multi_attempt`, do not assume a snippet worked just because `diagnostics: []` is empty. Line-scoping quirks mean errors sometimes attach to the enclosing by block. You MUST check that goals_after is empty (or has advanced) to confirm success.
98	- Beware `lean_run_code` with imports: Standalone `lean_run_code` snippets can sometimes silently swallow elaboration errors if they contain import statements. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
99	
100	## Logging
101	
102	Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Use the file name from your assigned `.lean` file (e.g., if you own `Algebra/WLocal.lean`, write to `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`).
103	
104	**Format:**
105	
106	```markdown
107	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
108	
109	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
110	### Attempt 1
111	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
112	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance not available
113	- **Dead end:** Do not try direct case split without IsLocalRing
114	
115	### Attempt 2
116	- **Approach:** Use Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
117	- **Result:** RESOLVED
118	- **Key insight:** Mathlib's PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective bridges the gap
119	
120	## helper_bijective (line 78)
121	### Attempt 1
122	- **Approach:** Split conjunction into injectivity and surjectivity. Prove injectivity.
123	- **Result:** PARTIAL (Branch Closed) — Injectivity closed. Sorry count unchanged because surjectivity remains.
124	- **Next step:** Try PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective for the right branch.
125	- **Relevant lemmas found:** PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective
126	```
127	
128	**Rules:**
129	1. One section per theorem/lemma in your file
130	2. Each attempt records: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps
131	3. Log negative search results (e.g., "Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib")
132	4. The plan agent collects these files and merges them into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`
133	
134	**Read-only references:** Read `task_pending.md` for prior context on your file. Read `task_done.md` if the current problem resembles a completed one. Do not write to either.
135	
136	## Summary Pipeline
137	
138	1. Read `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md` for context from prior sessions
139	2. Read the informal proof / blueprint to understand the proof strategy and lemma decomposition
140	3. Introduce helper lemmas (matching the blueprint's structure) in the `.lean` file
141	4. Replace `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs, ensuring the file compiles without errors
142	5. Do not modify protected declarations listed in `archon-protected.yaml`.
143	6. Use Mathlib theorems when possible. Use Web Search when Mathlib lacks referenced results
144	7. Rely on Lean LSP for diagnostics; use `lake env lean <file>` sparingly for final checks
145	8. Log all explorations in `task_results/<your_file>.md`
146	
147	## End-of-session handoff
148	
149	Before you stop (or when you are done with this round of work):
150	
151	1. Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` with:
152	   - Current result (IN PROGRESS / FAILED) and what you tried
153	   - Any Mathlib lemmas you discovered that are relevant
154	   - Concrete next step for the next session
155	   - Dead-end warnings for approaches that won't work
156	2. Save all file changes (ensure compilation passes, using scoped `sorry` if needed)
157

1	# Project Progress
2	
3	## Current Stage
4	
5	prover (iteration 108 — single substantive prover lane; close
6	`h_loc_exact` at L1783 of `BasicOpenCech.lean` via the mathlib-analogist-
7	iter106 Q1 ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB recipe. Iter-107 lane on
8	`cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` (L1120) PAUSED per progress-critic-iter106
9	STUCK + strategy-critic-iter106 CHALLENGE: 7 consecutive PARTIAL iters
10	on that slot with the same root cause; mathlib-analogist confirmed the
11	post-hoc R-linearity certification on `ModuleCat k` is a self-imposed
12	Mathlib gap. The iter-108 substantive route pivots Phase A to L1783 —
13	structurally independent of L1120's anonymous-closure Pi.lift blocker —
14	where the Mathlib idiom `IsLocalizedModule.pi` + `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen`
15	+ `LinearEquiv.exact_iff_exact` provides a concrete ~80–120 LOC recipe.)
16	
17	## Stages
18	- [x] init
19	- [x] autoformalize
20	- [ ] prover
21	- [ ] polish
22	
23	## Big-picture context
24	
25	This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible
26	curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`).
27	The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables.

95	### 1. `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` — close `h_loc_exact` at L1783 via the mathlib-analogist Q1 ALIGN recipe
96	
97	**File status (iter-108 entry).** 6 sorries; FILE COMPILES.
98	
99	`h_loc_exact` is a body-local `have` inside `basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf` (L1170), within the `h_K₀_exact` block (L1544). It claims per-`f ∈ s₀` exactness of the LOCALISED Čech differential of K₀ at degree n:
100	
101	```lean
102	have h_loc_exact (f : ↑s₀) : Function.Exact
103	    ⇑(LocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers (f.1 : R)) f_R)
104	    ⇑(LocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers (f.1 : R)) g_R) := sorry
105	```
106	
107	The proof closes `h_K₀_exact` via `exact_of_localized_span (↑s₀ : Set R) h_top f_R g_R h_loc_exact` at L1786 (Mathlib's local-to-global exactness). Upstream context: `R := Γ(C.left, U)`, `scK₀ := HomologicalComplex.sc K₀ n`, `f_R, g_R : scK₀.X_i →ₗ[R] scK₀.X_{i+1}` are the R-linear repackagings of the K₀ Čech differential; `h_a₀_fun f` (sorry-routed via `h_a₀ f`, in scope at L1565+) provides slice-cover exactness at degree n.
108	
109	### Recipe (mathlib-analogist Q1 ALIGN, ~80–120 LOC bounded)
110	
111	Mathlib-aligned closure recipe — every named lemma is [verified] by the analogist's read of `Mathlib b80f227`:
112	
113	#### Step 1. Per-coord `IsLocalization.Away` on basic-open identification
114	
115	For each `f ∈ ↑s₀` and each `x : Fin (n+1) → ↑s₀`:
116	
117	a. Let `V_x := ∏ᶜ_a basicOpenCover (↑s₀ : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (x a)` (the K₀ cochain factor at degree n, position x). `V_x` is affine by [verified] `basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isAffineOpen` (project-local L261). It satisfies `V_x ≤ U`.
118	
119	b. The image `f.1|_{V_x} : Γ(C.left, V_x)` of `f.1 : R` under the restriction algebra map `R → Γ(C.left, V_x)` has basic-open `V_x ⊓ D(f.1) = D(f.1|_{V_x})` ([verified] `Scheme.basicOpen_res`).
120	
121	c. Apply [verified] `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen` (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean:716`) on `(V_x, f.1|_{V_x})` to get `IsLocalization.Away (f.1|_{V_x}) Γ(C.left, V_x ⊓ D(f.1))`. The algebra structure is `(C.left.presheaf.map _.op).hom.toAlgebra`.
122	
123	#### Step 2. Per-coord `IsLocalizedModule` bridge
124	
125	For each `x` and `f`, apply [verified] `instIsLocalizedModuleToLinearMapToAlgHomOfIsLocalizationAlgebraMapSubmonoid` (`Mathlib.Algebra.Module.LocalizedModule.IsLocalization`) to upgrade Step 1c's `IsLocalization.Away` into an `IsLocalizedModule (powers f.1|_{V_x})` instance on the algebra map `Γ(C.left, V_x) → Γ(C.left, V_x ⊓ D(f.1))` viewed as a LinearMap.
126	
127	#### Step 3. Apply `IsLocalizedModule.pi` for the finite-product instance
128	
129	The K₀ cochain factor at degree n is `scK₀.X_i = ∏ᶜ_x V_x`-module. Apply [verified] `IsLocalizedModule.pi` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.TensorProduct.IsBaseChangePi:93`) to obtain `IsLocalizedModule (powers f.1) (LinearMap.pi <per-coord restrictions>)`. Finite-index `Fin (n+1) → ↑s₀` is `Finite` by [expected] `Fintype.toFinite`.
130	
131	This is the key Mathlib idiom — the project does NOT need to build the product-localisation commutation by hand. The analogist explicitly identified this as the alignment gap iter-069 missed.
132	
133	#### Step 4. Bridge `LocalizedModule.map ... f_R` to `LinearMap.pi <restriction>` ∘ `f_R`
134	
135	The goal's morphism `LocalizedModule.map (powers f.1) f_R : LocalizedModule (powers f.1) scK₀.X₁ → LocalizedModule (powers f.1) scK₀.X₂` is the canonical lift of `f_R` to the canonical `LocalizedModule (powers f.1)`. By [verified] `IsLocalizedModule.iso` (`Mathlib.Algebra.Module.LocalizedModule.Basic`), there is a `LinearEquiv LocalizedModule (powers f.1) M ≃ₗ[R] M'` for any M' satisfying `IsLocalizedModule (powers f.1)`. Apply this on M = scK₀.X_i / M' = the LinearMap.pi codomain from Step 3 — gives a LinearEquiv from `LocalizedModule (powers f.1) scK₀.X_i` to the slice-cover degree-i term (modulo the `ModuleCat.piIsoPi` repackaging already in scope as `e_i` at L1615–L1617).
136	
137	By [verified] `LinearEquiv.exact_iff_exact` (or its Mathlib-search synonym), `Function.Exact f g ↔ Function.Exact (e ∘ f ∘ e.symm) (e' ∘ g ∘ e'.symm)` for appropriate LinearEquivs e, e'. Use this to transport `h_a₀_fun f` (slice-cover degree-n exactness) to the goal's `LocalizedModule.map`-form exactness.

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.MayerVietorisCover
7	
8	/-!
9	# Basic-open cover wrappers and the substantive Čech acyclicity theorem
10	
11	This file (iter-063 split, cohort 3) contains the `basicOpenCover` family
12	of definitions and identification lemmas (iter-054), the producer
13	`hasAffineCechAcyclicCover_of_basicOpen` (iter-055), the
14	`cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt` lemma, and the
15	substantive theorem `basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`
16	(iter-056 → iter-062). The latter currently carries two labelled substep
17	sorries plus the iter-062 `h_a_fun` scaffolding.
18	
19	This is cohort 3 of the original `MayerVietoris.lean` split:
20	* `MayerVietorisCore.lean`: MV LES core + Mathlib gap-fill.
21	* `MayerVietorisCover.lean`: 2-affine cover MV + cover-totality bridges +
22	  `IsCechAcyclicCover` consumers + `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` carrier.
23	* `BasicOpenCech.lean` (this file): basic-open cover wrappers + the
24	  substantive Čech acyclicity theorem.
25	
26	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex`.
27	-/
28	
29	set_option autoImplicit false
30	
31	universe u v
32	
33	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
34	
35	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
36	
37	/-- Iter-054: basic-open cover from a spanning subset of `Γ(C.left, U)`.
38	
39	Given a subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`, this constructs the family of basic opens
40	`s → Opens` mapping each `f ∈ s` to `C.left.basicOpen f`. When `s` spans the
41	unit ideal of `Γ(C.left, U)` and `U` is affine, this family covers `U`
42	(see `basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top`); each member is itself an affine
43	open (see `basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen`).
44	
45	Iter-054 is a thin Mathlib-API wrapper (parameterised by the project's
46	`C : Over (Spec (.of k))` convention) so iter-055+ instantiation work for
47	`HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)` can call the constructor +
48	supremum + affine-membership directly.
49	
50	Mirrors the iter-047 foundational parameterised Čech infrastructure pattern:
51	package thin Mathlib wrappers as named project-local declarations so the
52	substantive multi-iteration work downstream focuses purely on content
53	(Koszul acyclicity + Čech-vs-derived comparison) rather than re-deriving
54	infrastructure at every call site. -/
55	noncomputable def basicOpenCover
56	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
57	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
58	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) :
59	    s → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat :=
60	  fun f => C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U))
61	
62	/-- Iter-054: supremum equality for the basic-open cover from generators
63	spanning the unit ideal of an affine open.
64	
65	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)` and `(hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤)`, the basic-open
66	cover `basicOpenCover s` covers `U` exactly: `⨆ i, basicOpenCover s i = U`.
67	This is a thin wrapper around Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff`
68	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean` L898).
69	
70	Used by iter-055+ when constructing the `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)`
71	instance: the existential's third conjunct `(⨆ i, 𝒰 i = U)` is exactly what
72	this theorem produces. -/
73	theorem basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top
74	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
75	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
76	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤) :
77	    ⨆ i, basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i = U := by
78	  unfold basicOpenCover
79	  exact hU.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff.mpr hs
80	
81	/-- Iter-054: each member of the basic-open cover of an affine open is itself
82	affine.
83	
84	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)` and `(i : s)`, conclude `IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover s i)`.
85	This is a thin wrapper around Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.basicOpen`
86	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean` L589).
87	
88	Used by iter-055+ when proving the Koszul acyclicity of the basic-open Čech
89	complex: each finite intersection of basic opens is itself basic (hence affine),
90	which is the inductive step for the Koszul-style alternating-sum argument. -/
91	theorem basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen
92	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
93	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
94	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (i : s) :
95	    IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i) := by
96	  unfold basicOpenCover
97	  exact hU.basicOpen i.1
98	
99	/-- Iter-055: producer for `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` from existence of basic-open
100	Čech-acyclic cover.
101	
102	Given that for every affine open `U` of `C.left` there exists a spanning subset
103	`s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)` with `Ideal.span s = ⊤` such that the basic-open cover
104	`basicOpenCover s : s → Opens` carries both `IsCechAcyclicCover F (basicOpenCover s)`
105	and `HasCechToHModuleIso F (basicOpenCover s)`, conclude `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover F`.
106	
107	Iter-055 specialises iter-053's existence-bundled cover form to the basic-open cover
108	shape constructed iter-054. Strategic value: factors out the existence-bundling step
109	from iter-056+ substantive work, so the iter-056+ Koszul acyclicity branch and the
110	iter-057+ Čech-vs-derived comparison branch can each prove specifically the basic-open
111	form (which the universal Koszul + comparison arguments naturally produce), and the
112	iter-055 producer then bundles their results into iter-053's carrier shape automatically.
113	
114	Body: destructures the per-affine existence to obtain `(s, hs, hacy, hcomp)`, then
115	bundles them into iter-053's existential cover via iter-054's
116	`basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top` to produce the supremum hypothesis. -/
117	theorem hasAffineCechAcyclicCover_of_basicOpen
118	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
119	    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)}
120	    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]

1	# Analogy: finite-product localisation and post-hoc R-linearity of the Čech differential
2	
3	## Slug
4	finite-prod-loc
5	
6	## Iteration
7	106
8	
9	## Questions
10	
11	(Q1) Does Mathlib have a product-localisation commutation
12	`LocalizedModule (powers a) (∏ᶜ_x M_x) ≅ ∏ᶜ_x LocalizedModule (powers a) M_x`
13	for finite products in `ModuleCat k` (or `ModuleCat R`), and the matching
14	`IsLocalizedModule.Pi` typeclass instance? If yes, name them; if no,
15	characterize the gap.
16	
17	(Q2) Is there a Mathlib idiom for proving R-linearity of an alternating
18	sum of categorical morphisms `∑ (-1)^i • (Pi.lift_thing_i ≫ eqToHom)`
19	that avoids the per-summand pattern-match-on-anonymous-closure approach?
20	For example a `Preadditive` / `Linear k` -level lemma, or a `Cech` /
21	`simplicialNerve` instance, or a project-side reformulation that makes
22	R-linearity flow from typeclass structure rather than an explicit
23	alternating-sum proof?
24	
25	## Project artifact(s)
26	
27	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean:1683-1786` —
28	  `h_K₀_exact` block: `f_R`, `g_R`, `h_loc_X_i`, `h_loc_exact` (L1783
29	  sorry), followed by `exact_of_localized_span` to close `h_K₀_exact`.
30	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean:836-1121` — body of
31	  `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` with the iter-099/103/107 scaffold; L1120
32	  sorry preceded by `have h_iter104 := cechCofaceMap_summand_family_R_linear hU s₀ n hn i r' y'`.
33	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean:454-477` —
34	  `cechCofaceMap_summand_family` (named morphism family, iter-102).
35	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean:494-595` —
36	  `cechCofaceMap_summand_family_R_linear` (50-LOC binder-level proof of
37	  per-summand R-linearity, closed iter-104).
38	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean:180-228` —
39	  `toModuleKPresheaf : (Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k` and
40	  its sheaf bundling `toModuleKSheaf`.
41	
42	## Decisions identified
43	
44	### Decision Q1a: product-localisation commutation `IsLocalizedModule.pi`
45	
46	- **Mathlib idiom**: `IsLocalizedModule.pi`. Cite:
47	  `Mathlib.RingTheory.TensorProduct.IsBaseChangePi:93`.
48	
49	  ```lean
50	  instance pi {ι : Type*} [Finite ι]
51	      {M M' : ι → Type*} [∀ i, AddCommMonoid (M i)] [∀ i, AddCommMonoid (M' i)]
52	      [∀ i, Module R (M i)] [∀ i, Module R (M' i)]
53	      (f : ∀ i, M i →ₗ[R] M' i) [∀ i, IsLocalizedModule S (f i)] :
54	      IsLocalizedModule S (.pi fun i ↦ f i ∘ₗ .proj i)
55	  ```
56	
57	  Plus the binary sibling `IsLocalizedModule.prodMap` at L77 of the
58	  same file. Both are derived from
59	  `IsBaseChange.pi` / `IsBaseChange.prodMap` via the
60	  `isLocalizedModule_iff_isBaseChange` adapter, which means the
61	  underlying `IsLocalizedModule (LinearMap.pi …)` instance is
62	  definitionally a base-change statement — exactly the universal
63	  property "localisation commutes with finite products".
64	
65	  Why Mathlib chose this: localisation = base change against
66	  `Localization S`; base change of modules commutes with finite limits
67	  in `ModuleCat R` (a categorical fact); finite products in `ModuleCat
68	  R` are the dependent `∀ i, M' i`-types via `ModuleCat.piIsoPi`. The
69	  instance is a typeclass so consumers don't have to invoke it by hand.
70	
71	- **Project's current path**: the iter-069 docstring at
72	  `BasicOpenCech.lean:1583` describes "the
73	  product-localisation commutation `LocalizedModule (powers f) (∏ᶜ_x M_x) ≅
74	  ∏ᶜ_x LocalizedModule (powers f) M_x` holds". The sorry at L1783
75	  (`h_loc_exact`) is supposed to be closed by this commutation plus
76	  `h_a₀_fun f`. As of iter-106 no call site has been written.
77	
78	- **Gap**: identical (modulo `LinearMap.pi`-vs-`Pi.lift` repackaging).
79	  The Mathlib instance covers the `LinearMap.pi`-flavoured product;
80	  bridging to `Limits.Pi.lift`-flavoured product in `ModuleCat k`
81	  proceeds via `ModuleCat.piIsoPi : (∏ᶜ Z) ≅ ModuleCat.of k (∀ i, Z i)`
82	  (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Products`), which the project
83	  already uses (the `e₁`, `e₂`, `e₃` `LinearEquiv` handles at
84	  `BasicOpenCech.lean:1615-1617`).
85	
86	- **Cost of divergence (if project rebuilds the pi-localisation instead
87	  of importing)**: redundant ~50-LOC infrastructure that Mathlib already
88	  ships. None — just align.
89	
90	- **Verdict**: ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB.
91	
92	### Decision Q1b: per-coord localisation premise
93	
94	- **Mathlib idiom**: two-step bridge.
95	  1. `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen`
96	     (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean:716`) gives
97	     `IsLocalization.Away f Γ(X, V)` whenever `V = X.basicOpen f` and
98	     `V ≤ U` for affine `U` (algebra structure installed via
99	     `(X.presheaf.map i.op).hom.toAlgebra`).
100	  2. `instIsLocalizedModuleToLinearMapToAlgHomOfIsLocalizationAlgebraMapSubmonoid`
101	     (`Mathlib.Algebra.Module.LocalizedModule.IsLocalization`) upgrades
102	     an `IsLocalization` algebra map to an `IsLocalizedModule` instance
103	     on the algebra's `toLinearMap`.
104	
105	- **Project's current path**: the iter-069 docstring lists
106	  "Step 3: for each `f ∈ ↑s₀`, identify the localisation of `K₀.X i` at
107	  `f` with the `↑s₀`-indexed slice-cover term at the same degree" and
108	  uses `localizedModuleIsLocalizedModule` (L1768-1775) as a *stand-in*
109	  by working with `LocalizedModule` directly rather than going via the
110	  slice cover. This means the project has bypassed the natural
111	  per-coord identification with `Γ(V_x ⊓ D(f.1))`.
112	
113	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost. The current project path sidesteps the
114	  slice-cover identification, so `h_loc_exact`'s closure cannot
115	  consume `h_a₀_fun f` directly — there's no Mathlib lemma
116	  ``IsLocalizedModule.linearMap_pi → LocalizedModule.map (...) f_R``
117	  that bypasses the slice-cover identification.
118	
119	  The bridge has to go: `slice-cover.f at coord n` (R-linear in
120	  `ModuleCat k`) = `LinearMap.pi (per-coord restriction)` ∘
121	  `K₀.f at coord n` (under suitable identification), then identify
122	  `LocalizedModule.map (powers f.1) f_R` with the LHS via
123	  `IsLocalizedModule.iso` and uniqueness.
124	
125	- **Cost of divergence**: zero new Mathlib gap, but ~50 LOC of project
126	  glue to build the per-coord algebra structures, install the
127	  `IsLocalization.Away` instances on the slice factors, and bridge to
128	  the `IsLocalizedModule` form via the Mathlib adapter.
129	
130	- **Verdict**: ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB (the iter-069 docstring already
131	  outlines this; the project just needs to write it).
132	
133	### Decision Q1c: transporting `Function.Exact` across the localisation iso
134	
135	- **Mathlib idiom**: `IsLocalizedModule.iso`
136	  (`Mathlib.Algebra.Module.LocalizedModule.Basic`) gives
137	  `LocalizedModule S M ≃ₗ[R] M'` whenever `IsLocalizedModule S f`. The
138	  pair `IsLocalizedModule.linearMap_ext` / `IsLocalizedModule.ext`
139	  provides extensionality. For `Function.Exact` transport across a
140	  `LinearEquiv`, `LinearEquiv.exact_iff_exact` (search: `LinearEquiv +
141	  Function.Exact`) is the canonical move.
142	
143	- **Project's current path**: not yet written; the sorry at L1783 is
144	  raw.
145	
146	- **Gap**: identical to Mathlib's intended use. No divergence.
147	
148	- **Verdict**: PROCEED (no Mathlib precedent dictates the exact glue
149	  shape; the project just writes it).
150	
151	### Decision Q2a: post-hoc R-linearity certification
152	
153	- **Mathlib idiom**: the canonical idiom in Mathlib is to **build the
154	  complex in the right category in the first place** rather than
155	  certify R-linearity post hoc. `cechComplexFunctor : (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤
156	  CochainComplex A ℕ` (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.SheafCohomology.Cech:65`)
157	  works for any `A` with the requisite limits — in particular for
158	  `A := ModuleCat R`, giving a Čech complex of **R-modules** whose
159	  differentials are R-linear by construction.
160	
161	  `CategoryTheory.Linear R (ModuleCat R)` is canonical: hom-sets in
162	  `ModuleCat R` ARE the R-modules of R-linear maps; composition and
163	  addition commute with the R-action by definition.
164	
165	  But `Linear R (ModuleCat k)` does **NOT** hold for `R ≠ k` in
166	  general: a `k`-linear map between `R`-modules-over-`k` need not be
167	  R-linear. There is no `Linear R`-instance on `ModuleCat k`, nor any
168	  Mathlib lemma "alternating sum of R-linear maps in
169	  `Hom_{ModuleCat k}(M, N)` is R-linear", because the hom-set isn't an
170	  R-module to start with.
171	
172	  `AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex` lives over
173	  `Preadditive C` (`Mathlib.AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex`)
174	  and inherits nothing more — it neither requires nor exposes a
175	  `Linear R`-structure.
176	
177	- **Project's current path**: build `cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰`
178	  in `CochainComplex (ModuleCat k) ℕ` and prove R-linearity of the
179	  differential post hoc via the iter-099+ alternating-sum scaffolding
180	  (`alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux`, `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp`,
181	  `alternating_zsmul_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp`, the named family
182	  `cechCofaceMap_summand_family` + its R-linearity at iter-102/104).
183	
184	  The 7-iter-stuck blocker at L1120 is *bridging* the iter-104
185	  binder-level R-linearity proof to the call-site anonymous-closure
186	  form of the K₀ differential.
187	
188	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost. Mathlib's idiom is "build in
189	  `ModuleCat R` if you need R-linearity"; the project's `ModuleCat k`
190	  + post-hoc-certify path has produced 7 consecutive failed iters
191	  because the categorical infrastructure (`Pi.lift`, alternating sum,
192	  `eqToHom`) is precisely tuned to preserve `Preadditive`/`Linear k`
193	  structure but obstinately whnf-reduces anonymous closures in ways
194	  that defeat the elaborator when tactics try to extract per-summand
195	  R-linearity.
196	
197	  The cost is *exactly* the 7 stuck iters: post-hoc R-linearity
198	  certification on a `ModuleCat k`-valued Čech differential is a
199	  Mathlib gap **created by the project's design choice**, not a
200	  pre-existing Mathlib gap that needs filling.
201	
202	- **Verdict**: ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB at the architectural level. Two
203	  feasible paths (see Recommendation below).
204	
205	### Decision Q2b: tactical bridging via `change` to named family
206	
207	- **Mathlib idiom**: no Mathlib precedent for this micro-level
208	  reformulation (Mathlib doesn't run into it because of Q2a's
209	  architectural choice).
210	
211	- **Project's current path**: the iter-099 attack route through
212	  `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp`/`alternating_zsmul_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp`
213	  tries to apply the structural lemma at the *call site* via
214	  Miller-pattern unification, with `G := fun i ↦ (anonymous closure)`
215	  baked into the `?G` placeholder. The unifier fails because the
216	  anonymous closure under whnf has nested `Pi.lift` bodies that the
217	  elaborator cannot decompose.
218	
219	- **Gap**: divergent-and-wrong (in the sense that the project keeps
220	  pursuing this route despite consistent failure).
221	
222	- **Cost**: 7 consecutive stuck iters and counting.
223	
224	- **Verdict**: PROCEED only if a fundamentally different micro-tactic
225	  (e.g. body-level `set F := cechCofaceMap_summand_family s₀ n` followed
226	  by `change ... = ∑ i ∈ Finset.univ, (-1)^↑i • F i ≫ eqToHom ⋯` at the
227	  *top* of the body — before any of the unfolds at L998-L1007 — so
228	  that the named family is what the unfolds reduce against) is
229	  attempted. **If this also fails, fall back to Q2a Path A
230	  (architectural refactor)**.
231	
232	## Recommendation
233	
234	### For Q1 (`h_loc_exact` sorry at BasicOpenCech.lean:1783)
235	
236	**ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB**. The closure recipe is:
237	
238	```
239	1. For each f ∈ s₀ and each x : Fin (n+1) → ↑s₀:
240	   a. V_x := ∏ᶜ_a basicOpenCover ↑s₀ (x a) is affine
241	      (basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isAffineOpen, iter-057).
242	   b. V_x ⊓ D(f.1) = D(f.1|_{V_x}) where f.1|_{V_x} is the
243	      image of f.1 under R = Γ(C.left, U) → Γ(C.left, V_x).
244	   c. IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen on (V_x, f.1|_{V_x})
245	      gives IsLocalization.Away (f.1|_{V_x}) Γ(C.left, V_x ⊓ D(f.1)).
246	   d. instIsLocalizedModuleToLinearMapToAlgHomOfIsLocalizationAlgebraMapSubmonoid
247	      upgrades to IsLocalizedModule (powers f.1) of the algebra-induced
248	      LinearMap Γ(V_x) → Γ(V_x ⊓ D(f.1)).
249	2. IsLocalizedModule.pi packages these per-coord instances into
250	   IsLocalizedModule (powers f.1) of LinearMap.pi(per-coord-restriction),
251	   for free as a typeclass instance.
252	3. Identify this LinearMap.pi with the slice-cover Čech differential
253	   at degree n by extensionality (LinearMap.pi_ext' is one option).
254	4. Use IsLocalizedModule.iso + LinearEquiv.exact_iff to transport
255	   h_a₀_fun f (slice-cover exactness) to exactness of the localised
256	   K₀ differential.
257	```
258	
259	**LOC**: ~80-120 LOC of glue. Strategy-critic's ~80 LOC estimate is
260	valid at the low end of this range — bridging step 1d (per-coord
261	algebra + IsLocalization.Away + adapter) is the bulk of the work.
262	
263	### For Q2 (`cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` sorry at BasicOpenCech.lean:1120)
264	
265	**MATHLIB_GAP_CONFIRMED via the project's choice of `ModuleCat k`**. Two
266	viable paths:
267	
268	**Path B (tactical, ~30-50 LOC, RECOMMENDED FIRST)**: at the very top
269	of `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul`'s body — **before** the
270	`dsimp only [scK₀, K₀, …]` at L998 — `set` the named family:
271	
272	```lean
273	let F := cechCofaceMap_summand_family s₀ n
274	```
275	
276	then after the dsimp+simp unfolds at L998-L1007, attempt a single
277	`change`-pivot rewriting the goal's `∑ i, (-1)^↑i • Pi.lift_thing_anonymous_i ≫ eqToHom`
278	into `∑ i, (-1)^↑i • F i ≫ eqToHom`. Because `F` was defined as the
279	named extraction of that same `Pi.lift_thing`, the equality is by
280	`rfl` at the elaborator level — provided the dsimp+simp chain hasn't

362	noncomputable def splitEpi_pi_lift_of_injective {k : Type u} [Field k]
363	    {α β : Type u} (M : α → ModuleCat.{u} k)
364	    (f : β → α) (hf : Function.Injective f) :
365	    SplitEpi (Pi.lift (fun b : β => Pi.π M (f b))) where
366	  section_ := by
367	    classical
368	    refine Pi.lift (fun a => ?_)
369	    by_cases h : ∃ b, f b = a
370	    · exact h.choose_spec ▸ Pi.π (fun b => M (f b)) h.choose
371	    · exact 0
372	  id := by
373	    classical
374	    apply Pi.hom_ext
375	    intro b
376	    rw [Category.assoc, Pi.lift_π, Category.id_comp, Pi.lift_π]
377	    have h : ∃ b', f b' = f b := ⟨b, rfl⟩
378	    rw [dif_pos h]
379	    -- The transport `h.choose_spec ▸ Pi.π _ h.choose = Pi.π _ b`.
380	    -- By injectivity of `f`, `h.choose = b`, so the transport collapses.
381	    -- We use the helper lemma below.
382	    have key : ∀ {b b' : β} (heq : f b' = f b),
383	        heq ▸ (Pi.π (fun b'' => M (f b'')) b' :
384	            (∏ᶜ fun b'' => M (f b'')) ⟶ M (f b')) =
385	          (Pi.π (fun b'' => M (f b'')) b :
386	            (∏ᶜ fun b'' => M (f b'')) ⟶ M (f b)) := by
387	      intro b b' heq
388	      have hbb : b' = b := hf heq
389	      subst hbb
390	      rfl
391	    exact key h.choose_spec

481	the domain (`∏ᶜ Z₁`-side) is the Pi.module structure with each factor's
482	restriction-algebra structure; the R-action on the codomain (`∏ᶜ Z_int`-side) is
483	similarly the Pi.module structure with each factor's restriction-algebra
484	structure. The morphism factors through

737	/-- Iter-096 (refactor extraction): structural lemma — if each summand
738	`F i` is R-linear (with respect to the LinearEquivs `e₁`, `e₂`), then
739	so is `∑ i ∈ s, F i`. The summand family `F : ι' → (M ⟶ N)` is named
740	abstractly, so applying this lemma to the Čech alternating sum
741	`∑ i, (-1)^↑i • Pi.lift fun i_1 ↦ ...` only requires Miller-pattern
742	unification `F := fun i ↦ <literal summand>` — no HOU on the
743	nested-i body.
744	
745	Body closed iter-097 via `Finset.cons_induction` on `s` with a simultaneous
746	`simp only` rewrite on both sides of the conclusion: the empty case collapses
747	through `Finset.sum_empty`, `ModuleCat.hom_zero`, `LinearMap.zero_apply`,
748	`map_zero`, and `smul_zero`; the cons step normalises the LHS/RHS in one pass
749	via `Finset.sum_cons`, `ModuleCat.hom_add`, `LinearMap.add_apply`, `map_add`,
750	then closes by chaining the per-summand hypothesis `hF i ⋯` on the head with
751	the induction hypothesis on the tail and a final `smul_add`. HOU-free because
752	`F` is a binder.
753	
754	**Refactor note (iter-096):** The directive also requested a companion
755	`cechCofaceMap_pi_smul_summand` that bakes the Čech summand literal
756	into its signature. That signature triggers a whnf timeout at the
757	`Pi.lift (fun x ↦ Pi.π (basicOpenCover ↑s₀ ∘ i_1) ((SimplexCategory.δ i)
758	.toOrderHom x))`-elaboration step, because Lean attempts to unify
759	`Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↥s₀` with `Fin n → ↥s₀` through
760	the heavy `ComplexShape.prev` definition. We omit that helper in iter-096;
761	the abstract structural lemma below suffices to give the iter-097 prover a
762	HOU-friendly hook (instantiate `F := fun i ↦ <Čech summand>` and discharge
763	per-summand R-linearity inline). -/
764	theorem alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux
765	    {k : Type u} [Field k]

1135	**Mathematical content (Stacks 01ED route).** For each positive degree `n`,
1136	exactness of the Čech cochain at degree `n` is a *local* property on the
1137	spanning set `s`. Localising the Čech complex at any `f ∈ s` exhibits the

import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.LocalizedModule.Basic

example {R : Type*} [CommSemiring R] (S : Submonoid R) {M : Type*} {N : Type*}
    [AddCommMonoid M] [AddCommMonoid N] [Module R M] [Module R N]
    (g : M →ₗ[R] N) :
    LocalizedModule.map S g =
      IsLocalizedModule.map S (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap S M)
        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap S N) g := by rfl

    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap S N) (Localization S)","informal_statement":"Given a commutative ring $R$ with a submonoid $S \subseteq R$, and $R$-modules $M$ and $N$, the function `LocalizedModule.map` takes an $R$-linear map $f \colon M \to N$ and returns an $R_S$-linear map between the localized modules $M_S \to N_S$, where $R_S$ is the localization of $R$ at $S$ and $M_S$, $N_S$ are the corresponding localized modules. This map is constructed by extending the scalars of $f$ from $R$ to $R_S$."},{"full_name":"IsLocalizedModule.map","formal_statement":"definition IsLocalizedModule.map : (M →ₗ[R] N) →ₗ[R] (M' →ₗ[R] N') where
  toFun h := lift S f (g ∘ₗ h) (IsLocalizedModule.map_units g)
  map_add' h₁ h₂ := by
    apply IsLocalizedModule.ext S f (IsLocalizedModule.map_units g)
    simp only [lift_comp, LinearMap.add_comp, LinearMap.comp_add]
  map_smul' r h := by
    apply IsLocalizedModule.ext S f (IsLocalizedModule.map_units g)
    simp only [lift_comp, LinearMap.add_comp, LinearMap.comp_add, LinearMap.smul_comp,
      LinearMap.comp_smul, RingHom.id_apply]","informal_statement":"Given a commutative semiring $R$, multiplicative subsets $S \subseteq R$, and $R$-module homomorphisms $f \colon M \to M'$ and $g \colon N \to N'$ that are localization maps at $S$, there exists a linear map $\text{map} \colon (M \toₗ[R] N) \to (M' \toₗ[R] N')$ that sends any $R$-linear map $h \colon M \to N$ to the unique $R$-linear map $M' \to N'$ making the following diagram commute:
\[
\begin{tikzcd}
M \arrow[r, "f"] \arrow[d, "h"] & M' \arrow[d, "\text{map}(h)"] \\
N \arrow[r, "g"] & N'
\end{tikzcd}
\]
This map is itself $R$-linear in $h$."},{"full_name":"IsLocalizedModule.map_apply","formal_statement":"theorem IsLocalizedModule.map_apply (h : M →ₗ[R] N) (x) : map S f g h (f x) = g (h x) :=
  lift_apply S f (g ∘ₗ h) (IsLocalizedModule.map_units g) x","informal_statement":"For any $R$-linear map $h \colon M \to N$ and any element $x \in M$, the induced localization map $\text{map}(h)$ satisfies $\text{map}(h)(f(x)) = g(h(x))$, where $f \colon M \to M'$ and $g \colon N \to N'$ are localization maps at a multiplicative subset $S \subseteq R$."},{"full_name":"IsLocalizedModule.fromLocalizedModule","formal_statement":"definition IsLocalizedModule.fromLocalizedModule : LocalizedModule S M →ₗ[R] M' where
  toFun := fromLocalizedModule' S f
  map_add' := fromLocalizedModule'_add S f
  map_smul' r x := by rw [fromLocalizedModule'_smul, RingHom.id_apply]","informal_statement":"Given a commutative semiring $R$, a multiplicative subset $S \subseteq R$, an $R$-module $M$, and a module homomorphism $f: M \to M'$ that satisfies the universal property of localization (i.e., $(M', f)$ is a localized module at $S$), there exists a canonical linear map from the localized module $\text{LocalizedModule}_S M$ to $M'$ as $R$-modules. This map sends an element represented as $m/s$ (where $m \in M$, $s \in S$) to $s^{-1}f(m)$ in $M'$, and is both additive and $R$-linear."},{"full_name":"IsLocalizedModule.mk'","formal_statement":"definition IsLocalizedModule.mk' (m : M) (s : S) : M' :=
  fromLocalizedModule S f (LocalizedModule.mk m s)","informal_statement":"Given a commutative semiring $R$, a multiplicative subset $S \subseteq R$, an $R$-module $M$, and a module homomorphism $f: M \to M'$ that satisfies the universal property of localization, the function $\text{mk}'$ sends a pair $(m, s) \in M \times S$ to the element $f(m)/s$ in $M'$, where $f(m)/s$ is represented via the canonical linear map from the localized module $\text{LocalizedModule}_S M$ to $M'$."}]}

for all $s \in S$ and $m \in M$, where $\otimes$ denotes the tensor product over $R$ via $f$."}]}

## `h_loc_exact` at L1783 (now L1802 after partial-proof inline)

### Attempt 1 — geometric setup for the analogist Q1 recipe
- **Approach.** Followed the iter-108 PROGRESS.md recipe (analogist Q1 ALIGN, from
  `analogies/finite-product-localisation-and-cech-r-linearity.md`). The recipe is:
  1. Per-coord `IsLocalization.Away (f.1|V_x) Γ(V_x ⊓ D(f.1))`
     via `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen` + `Scheme.basicOpen_res`.
  2. `IsLocalization` → `IsLocalizedModule` via
     `instIsLocalizedModuleToLinearMapToAlgHomOfIsLocalizationAlgebraMapSubmonoid`.
  3. `IsLocalizedModule.pi` (Mathlib `IsBaseChangePi:93`) for the finite product
     `Fin (n+1) → ↑s₀`.
  4. `IsLocalizedModule.iso` + `Function.Exact.iff_of_ladder_linearEquiv` transport
     `h_a₀_fun f` (slice-cover exactness) to the goal.
- **Result.** PARTIAL. Committed ~18 LOC of partial setup at L1781–L1802:
  - `h_V_le_U (x)`: per-coord `V_x = ∏ᶜ basicOpenCover ↑s₀ (x a) ≤ U`
    via `(Pi.π _ ⟨0, by omega⟩).le.trans (basicOpen_le _ _)`.
  - `h_slice_eq (x)`: slice-cover coord open equals basic open via
    `Scheme.basicOpen_res`: `V_x ⊓ D(f.1) = D(f.1|V_x)`.
  - Trailing `sorry` for Steps 1c, 2, 3, 4 (deferred — ~120 LOC of glue).
- **Key Mathlib lemmas verified [verified] this iter:**
  - `Function.Exact.iff_of_ladder_linearEquiv` (correct name for Step 4 transport).
  - `LinearEquiv.conj_exact_iff_exact` (alternative one-iso variant).
  - `IsLocalizedModule.map_iso_commute` (commutes `LocalizedModule.map` with
    `IsLocalizedModule.iso`).
  - `Submonoid.map_powers` (for `algebraMapSubmonoid (powers f.1) = powers (f.1|V_x)`).
  - `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact` exists in Mathlib at
    `Mathlib.Algebra.Module.LocalizedModule.Exact` but is **circular** in this
    context (requires `Function.Exact f_R g_R` = `h_K₀_exact`).
- **Next step (iter-109).** Lift the trailing sorry by writing Steps 1c-4 inline:
  1. Bind `letI := ((C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE (h_V_le_U x)).op).hom).toAlgebra`
     to install `Algebra R Γ(V_x)`. Use `IsScalarTower R Γ(V_x) Γ(V_x ⊓ D(f.1))`
     bookkeeping (parallel to the existing iter-072 pattern at L1717-L1725).
  2. Apply `hU.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen (f.1|V_x) _ (h_slice_eq x)` (after
     translating the algebraMap so f.1|V_x is the image in Γ(V_x)). This gives
     `IsLocalization.Away (f.1|V_x) Γ(V_x ⊓ D(f.1))`.
  3. Use `Submonoid.map_powers` to rewrite `algebraMapSubmonoid Γ(V_x) (powers f.1)
     = powers (f.1|V_x)`; then `IsLocalization.iff_of_eq` (if exists) or
     `IsLocalization.of_submonoid_eq` to switch submonoid.
  4. By `instIsLocalizedModuleToLinearMap...`, the algebra map
     `Γ(V_x) →ₗ[R] Γ(V_x ⊓ D(f.1))` is `IsLocalizedModule (powers f.1)`.
  5. By `IsLocalizedModule.pi`, the product `LinearMap.pi (per-coord restriction
     ∘ₗ Pi.proj)` is `IsLocalizedModule (powers f.1)`.
  6. Repackage `scK₀.X₁ = ∏ᶜ Z₁` and the K₀-to-slice restriction map as
     `LinearMap.pi`-form (use `ModuleCat.piIsoPi` = `e₁` already in scope).
  7. Identify the slice-cover.X_n with `∏ᶜ (Z_int : Fin (n+1) → ↑s₀ → ModuleCat k)`
     where `Z_int x = Γ(V_x ⊓ D(f.1))` (via `Scheme.cechCochain` + the slice
     cover's degree-`n` factor unfolding).
  8. By `IsLocalizedModule.iso`, get `e_loc_i : LocalizedModule (powers f.1) scK₀.X_i
     ≃ₗ[R] slice_cover.X_i (R-linearly viewed)`.
  9. Apply `Function.Exact.iff_of_ladder_linearEquiv` with the three e_loc_i and
     the commutation diagram `slice_cover.f at coord ∘ e_loc_1 = e_loc_2 ∘
     (LocalizedModule.map (powers f.1) f_R)`. The commutation is by uniqueness
     of `IsLocalizedModule.map` (via `IsLocalizedModule.linearMap_ext`).
 10. Use `h_a₀_fun f` (function-level slice-cover exactness already in scope)
     for the LHS of the iff.

### Architectural dead-ends ruled out
- **`LocalizedModule.map_exact` direct application** — CIRCULAR per analogist;
  requires `Function.Exact f_R g_R` = unlocalized K₀ exactness, which is exactly

- **Reuse of `h_loc_X_i` directly without slice-cover bridge** — `h_loc_X_i f`

  trivial (`localizedModuleIsLocalizedModule`); it contributes no slice-cover

Following the analogist's 80-120 LOC estimate. The geometric setup (`h_V_le_U`,
